title: Polymorphism
description: Master dynamic dispatch, virtual tables (Vtables), override/final keywords, and virtual destructors.
learningObjectives: Use virtual functions to enable dynamic (runtime) polymorphism
Explain the inner mechanics and cost of Virtual Table (Vtable) lookups
Identify and prevent memory leaks using virtual destructors

sections.title: Vtables and Dynamic Dispatch
sections.content:
Polymorphism lets you treat derived objects through base class pointers. Dynamic polymorphism uses `virtual` functions resolved at runtime. 

When a class has virtual functions, the compiler inserts a hidden pointer (`vptr`) into the object layout pointing to a Virtual Table (`vtable`). Calling a virtual function requires dereferencing the `vptr`, locating the function pointer offset, and calling it. This overhead (vtable lookup and cache miss risk) is why high-frequency tick systems avoid deep polymorphic loops.
